name: Maili Point
tagline: A low-key Waianae reef that delivers when you want to avoid the tourist trail entirely.
parking: Street parking along Farrington Hwy near Maili Beach Park
bestTime: Summer mornings; only snorkel when the ocean is calm — exposed reef with no protection
talkStory: Maili Point is a rocky outcrop with a reef that gets overlooked because tourists rarely venture this far down the Waianae Coast. On calm summer days the water along the point is clear with decent coral coverage and reef fish. Entry is rocky so wear reef shoes and pick your spot carefully. This is a local beach — respect the community, do not blast music, and clean up after yourself. Not worth the drive as a standalone trip but solid if you are already in the area.
